UG-36  OPENINGS IN PRESSURE VESSELS. (a) Openings in shells, heads, and other pressure boundary components are permitted, provided that the requirements of this paragraph and UG-37 are met. (b) The maximum diameter of unreinforced openings shall not exceed the limits specified herein. Openings exceeding these limits shall be reinforced in accordance with UG-37 and UG-38. (c) The shape of openings shall be circular, elliptical, or obround, except as permitted by the designer when adequate reinforcement is provided per UG-37. Nozzle neck thickness shall comply with UG-44. Limits of reinforcement shall be as specified in UG-40. For openings in flat heads, see also UG-35. Ligament efficiency between openings shall be checked in accordance with UW-13.
